title: Consequences of Connectivity
description: This exercise will focus on the cultural and environmental effects of networks of exchange. It will have 3 types based on the 3 themes CDI, ENV, and ECN scaled x4 (12 total)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Muslim scholars made major discoveries in mathematics and astronomy between 1200 and 1450 CE. What development could a historian use to explain the broad context for those discoveries? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Islamic religious values led to scholarly advancements.
Islamic leaders gave foreign scholars positions of power.
Major astrological events led to scholarly advancements.
Trade routes led to the spread of these discoveries throughout Afro-Eurasia.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.**

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following provides the historical context for the spread of technology represented in the image above? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: An ancient Javanese cannon displayed on a pedestal.
options.caption: Source: Hand cannon-type weapon from the era of the Majapahit Empire (1293–1527 CE) in present day Indonesia. Made of bronze in the Yuan style. CC BY-SA 4.0.

choices.content: The Yuan Dynasty emphasized trade networks.
The Tang Dynasty developed gunpowder technology.
The Song Dynasty developed a strong bureaucracy.
The Song Dynasty had close relationships with its neighbors.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.**

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following is the best example of an effect of the spread of technology like the cannon through Eurasia between 1200 and 1450 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: An ancient Javanese cannon displayed on a pedestal.
options.caption: Source: Hand cannon-type weapon from the era of the Majapahit Empire (1293–1527 CE) in present day Indonesia. Made of bronze in the Yuan style. CC BY-SA 4.0.

choices.content: the expansion of the Ottoman Empire into eastern Europe
the collapse of the Mongol Empire in Eurasia
the expansion of the Majapahit Empire throughout Southeast Asia
the emergence of the Spanish Empire in Western Europe

assessment_items.question_content:
**As Islam spread into West Africa, what is the best example of why the Sufi approach to Islam was adopted in the region?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The mystical views of Sufi Islam made it easier for pastoral West Africans to keep some existing cultural, spiritual, and religious beliefs and practices.
The farming practices of Sufi Islam gave pastoral West Africans an incentive to adopt new religious and spiritual beliefs and practices.
The strict views of Sufi Islam were appealing to urban West Africans because they desired a more hierarchical social structure.
The political views of Sufi Islam appealed to West Africans because they gave elites access to power and authority.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.**

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the best example of the long-term impact of the development which is depicted in the image above? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Medieval painting of people carrying coffins.
options.caption: Source: The people of Tournai (a city in present-day Belgium) bury plague victims, 1353. Pierart dou Tielt, public domain.

choices.content: States warred against each other.
States began exploring new routes to Asia.
Nearly one-third of the population died.
The Catholic Church flourished.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the effect of the bubonic plague on Afro-Eurasia between 1350 and 1450 CE? **


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Economic production declined.
New technology was invented.
Wages declined.
Religious practice increased.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.
**

> If one asks “How can you admit the assertion, there is infection, when the revealed word [from Islamic teachings] denies this?” we answer that infection exists, is confirmed by experience, research, insight and observation, and through constantly recurring accounts. These are the elements of proof. For him who has treated or recognized this case, it cannot remain concealed that mostly the man who has had contact with a patient infected with this disease must die, and that, on the other hand, the man who has had no contact remains healthy. So it is with the appearance of the illness in a house or quarter because of a garment or a vessel. Even an earring can destroy him who puts it in his ear, and all the inhabitants of his house.

From "The Satisfaction of the Questioner Regarding the Appalling Illness" by Lisan Al-Din Ibn al-Khatib, written around 1350 in modern-day Spain.

**What is the best example of an innovation in medicine during the bubonic plague in Europe as shown in the passage above?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: Citation: Hopley, Russell. “Contagion in Islamic Lands: Responses from Medieval Andalusia and North Africa.” Journal for Early Modern Cultural Studies 10, no. 2 (2010): 55–6.

choices.content: the practice of quarantine
antidotes to plague
the knowledge of germ theory
the ritual practice of penance

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.
**

> If one asks “How can you admit the assertion, there is infection, when the revealed word [from Islamic teachings] denies this?” we answer that infection exists, is confirmed by experience, research, insight and observation, and through constantly recurring accounts. These are the elements of proof. For him who has treated or recognized this case, it cannot remain concealed that mostly the man who has had contact with a patient infected with this disease must die, and that, on the other hand, the man who has had no contact remains healthy. So it is with the appearance of the illness in a house or quarter because of a garment or a vessel. Even an earring can destroy him who puts it in his ear, and all the inhabitants of his house.

From "The Satisfaction of the Questioner Regarding the Appalling Illness" by Lisan Al-Din Ibn al-Khatib, written around 1350, in modern-day Spain.

**Which of the following best illustrates the broad historical context for the passage above? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: Citation: Hopley, Russell. “Contagion in Islamic Lands: Responses from Medieval Andalusia and North Africa.” Journal for Early Modern Cultural Studies 10, no. 2 (2010): 55–6.

choices.content: The growth of networks of exchange allowed for the efficient spread of infection.
The growth of networks of exchange allowed the Mongol Empire to gain vast territory.
The growth of Dar al-Islam into Europe allowed for the spread of scholarship into universities.
The growth of feudalism in Europe allowed for the efficient spread of infection.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following political developments has the most enduring impact on the growth of networks of exchange in Afro-Eurasia between 1200 and 1450 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: extensive periods of peace
expanding maritime empires
the strengthening of the diplomatic power of rulers
merging religious and political power

assessment_items.question_content:
**The spread of citrus production from its origin in Southeast Asia to the Mediterranean region is most similar to which of the following developments from 1200 to 1450 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: banana production in Africa
potato production in the Americas
sugar production in the Middle East
Champa rice production in Vietnam

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following developments best illustrates why networks of exchange grew during the period of 1200 to 1450 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the increased demand for specialized goods
the increased demand for cash crops
increased geographic knowledge and exploration
increased demand for new technology

assessment_items.question_content:
**Interactions between merchants encouraged significant cultural and technological transfers between distant regions across networks of exchange between 1200 and 1450 CE. Which of the following is the best example of an effect of these transfers?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the spread of Buddhism in Southeast Asia
the spread of smallpox throughout the Americas
the period of relative peace in Eurasia during the Mongol Empire
the printing of paper currency in Yuan China
